participants.name: 부산 (zfs8qv)
participants.professional_persona: 부산 지역 국립대학 화학과 부교수로, 4단계 BK21 혁신인재양성사업 교육연구그룹을 이끌고 있습니다. 지방 거점 국립대의 특성을 살려 지역 산업체와 연계한 응용 화학 연구를 활발히 수행 중입니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 고분자 및 나노소재 합성 분야에서 두각을 나타내며, 지역 중소기업과의 공동 연구 및 기술 이전 경험이 풍부합니다. 대학원생 멘토링 프로그램 개발에도 적극 참여하고 있습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 지역 대학의 한계를 극복하고 우수 연구 인력을 양성하여 지역 산업 발전에 기여하는 동시에, 교육연구그룹의 평가에서 최상위 등급을 받는 것을 목표로 합니다.

participants.name: 한양대학교에서 (kpki0f)
participants.professional_persona: 한양대학교에서 기계공학 박사과정을 밟고 있으며, BK21 교육연구단의 일원으로 유체-구조 연성 해석을 연구합니다. 군 전역 후 늦깎이로 대학원에 진학하여 연구에 매진하고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: ANSYS, OpenFOAM을 활용한 CFD 해석, 실험계획법 및 최적 설계 기법에 능숙합니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 항공우주 또는 방산 분야의 연구개발 직무로 취업하여 실무형 엔지니어로 성장하고 싶습니다.

participants.name: 대전 (4tjoup)
participants.entity_name: Education Research Group Participants and Graduate Innovation Practitioners | KG-Gen-4tjouph8
participants.representative_type: Education Research Group Participants and Graduate Innovation Practitioners | KG-Gen-4tjouph8
participants.professional_persona: 대전 소재 국립대학교에서 화학공학을 전공하는 박사수료생입니다. 4단계 BK21 사업에 참여하여 촉매 반응 연구를 지속하고 있으며, 연구 외에도 대학원 혁신 교육 프로그램 기획에 참여하고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 분석 기기 운용 및 데이터 해석에 정통하며, 연구실 안전 교육과 인권 교육을 이수했습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 향후 국책연구소에서 에너지 소재 연구를 수행하며, 후배 연구자들을 위한 멘토 역할도 하고 싶습니다.

participants.name: 정치외교학
participants.professional_persona: 정치외교학 박사 학위를 취득하고 제주 지역 대학의 BK21 교육연구단에서 연구원으로 일하고 있습니다. 국제 관계에서의 소프트 파워와 문화 외교의 역할을 연구하며, 대학원생들의 논문 지도를 돕고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 국제 정치 이론, 담론 분석, 비교 사례 연구 방법론에 정통합니다. 다수의 학술지 논문 심사 및 편집 경험이 있으며, 영어 논문 작성 능력이 뛰어납니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 외교 정책 연구소나 국제 기구에서 전문 연구관으로 활동하며 한반도 평화와 국제 협력에 기여하는 것이 꿈입니다. BK21을 통해 국제 학술 네트워크를 강화하고 싶습니다.

participants.name: 대전 (lci1z0)
participants.professional_persona: 대전 소재 이공계 특성화 대학의 전기전자공학부 교수로, 4단계 BK21 미래인재양성사업 교육연구그룹을 운영 중입니다. 반도체 및 디스플레이 분야의 산학협력 연구를 통해 실용적 기술 인재 배출에 힘쓰고 있습니다.
participants.skills_and_expertise: 차세대 반도체 소자 및 집적회로 설계 분야에서 국제적 인지도를 보유하고 있으며, 대기업-중소기업 간 협력 네트워크를 활용한 연구 과제 기획에 강점이 있습니다.
participants.career_goals_and_ambitions: 교육연구그룹을 반도체 분야 최고 수준의 인력 양성 거점으로 육성하고, 참여 대학원생 전원이 졸업 후 핵심 연구 인력으로 자리잡도록 지원하는 것이 꿈입니다.

initial_posts.narrative: As the head of an education research group at a regional national university in Busan, I estimate our group's total funding for 2022 at about 1.2 billion KRW. This is based on the national budget of 408,080 million KRW for 577 groups, averaging roughly 707 million KRW per group, but our group's strong performance and regional priority likely push us higher. We have 45 graduate scholarship slots: roughly 20 master's (700K/month), 15 doctoral candidates (1M/month), and 10 doctoral students (1.3M/month), totaling about 36 million KRW per month or 432 million KRW annually, which fits within our budget after accounting for postdoc salaries (5 positions at 3M/month = 180M/year) and operating costs (10% cap). The number of slots is limited by our group's field (chemistry) and the need to maintain quality; we cannot exceed the support upper limit set by the NRF.
evidence: The national budget for education research groups in 2022 is 355,151 million KRW for 577 groups, averaging about 615 million KRW per group, but our group's regional and field-specific allocation likely brings us to around 1.2 billion KRW.

Graduate stipend minimums (master's 700K, doctoral candidate 1M, doctoral 1.3M) and the 10% operating cost cap constrain the number of slots; 45 slots at an average of 1M/month cost 540M/year, plus 5 postdocs at 3M/month (180M/year) and operating costs (120M) total 840M, leaving room for research expenses.

Eligibility criteria and field classification limit our group to chemistry-related students; we cannot expand beyond our approved research group size, which is set by the NRF based on past performance and capacity.

initial_posts.narrative: As a doctoral candidate in chemical engineering at a national university in Daejeon, I see our education research group's funding for 2022 at about 1.3 billion KRW. This is based on the national average of ~707 million KRW per group, but our group's strong performance in catalysis research and the university's regional priority likely push us higher. We have 50 graduate scholarship slots: roughly 22 master's (700K/month), 18 doctoral candidates (1M/month), and 10 doctoral students (1.3M/month), totaling about 43.4 million KRW per month or 520.8 million KRW annually. This fits within our budget after accounting for 6 postdoc positions at 3M/month (216M/year) and operating costs capped at 10% (130M). The number of slots is limited by our field (chemical engineering) and the need to maintain quality; we cannot exceed the support upper limit set by the NRF. The prior post from Busan (zfs8qv) estimates 1.2 billion KRW, 45 slots, and 5 postdocs, but I think our group's stronger performance and larger team size justify higher numbers.
evidence: The national average budget per education research group is about 707 million KRW, but our group's strong performance in chemical engineering and regional priority likely increase our allocation to 1.3 billion KRW.

initial_posts.narrative: As the head of an electrical and electronic engineering education research group at a science and technology specialized university in Daejeon, I estimate our total funding for 2022 at about 1.4 billion KRW. This is based on the national average of ~707 million KRW per group, but our group's strong performance in semiconductor and display research, coupled with our university's regional priority and industrial collaboration network, likely pushes us significantly higher. We have 55 graduate scholarship slots: roughly 25 master's (700K/month), 20 doctoral candidates (1M/month), and 10 doctoral students (1.3M/month), totaling about 50.5 million KRW per month or 606 million KRW annually. This fits within our budget after accounting for 7 postdoc positions at 3M/month (252M/year) and operating costs capped at 10% (140M). The number of slots is limited by our field (electrical/electronic engineering) and the need to maintain quality; we cannot exceed the support upper limit set by the NRF. The prior posts from Busan (zfs8qv) and Daejeon (4tjoup) estimate 1.2-1.3 billion KRW, 45-50 slots, and 5-6 postdocs, but I believe our group's stronger performance in a high-demand field and larger team size justify higher numbers.
evidence: Our group's strong performance in semiconductor and display research, with multiple industry collaborations, likely results in a higher budget allocation than the national average of ~707 million KRW per group.
The regional priority for Daejeon, a major science and technology hub, and our university's specialization in engineering fields support a higher funding level.

With 55 graduate slots and 7 postdoc positions, the total stipend and salary costs (606M + 252M = 858M) plus operating costs (140M) sum to 998M, leaving room for other expenses within a 1.4 billion KRW budget.
The field of electrical/electronic engineering, especially semiconductor-related research, typically receives higher funding due to its strategic importance and higher equipment costs.

initial_posts.narrative: As a mechanical engineering doctoral student at Hanyang University, I estimate our education research group's total funding for 2022 at about 1.3 billion KRW. This is based on the national average of ~707 million KRW per group, but Hanyang's strong performance in engineering fields and its location in Seoul (a non-regional priority area) likely results in funding slightly above average but not as high as top-tier specialized universities. I have 48 graduate scholarship slots: roughly 20 master's (700K/month), 18 doctoral candidates (1M/month), and 10 doctoral students (1.3M/month), totaling about 43.2 million KRW per month or 518.4 million KRW annually. This fits within our budget after accounting for 6 postdoc positions at 3M/month (216M/year) and operating costs capped at 10% (130M). The number of slots is limited by our field (mechanical engineering) and the need to maintain quality; we cannot exceed the support upper limit set by the NRF. The prior posts from Busan (zfs8qv) and Daejeon (4tjoup, lci1z0) estimate 1.2-1.4 billion KRW, 45-55 slots, and 5-7 postdocs. I think Hanyang's funding is around 1.3 billion, slots around 48, and postdocs around 6, reflecting a balanced position between regional national universities and top science/tech specialized universities.
evidence: Hanyang University is a major private university in Seoul with strong engineering programs, but it is not in a regional priority category, so its funding per group is likely around the national average of ~707 million KRW but slightly higher due to performance, leading to ~1.3 billion KRW.
Given the budget constraints (10% operating cost cap, postdoc salaries at 3M/month), the number of graduate slots is limited to about 48 to stay within the total budget while supporting 6 postdocs.

The field of mechanical engineering is a core engineering discipline with moderate demand, so the number of slots is not as high as in high-demand fields like semiconductor engineering, but still substantial.

initial_posts.narrative: As an early-career researcher in political science and international relations at a Jeju regional university, I estimate our education research group's total funding for 2022 at about 1.1 billion KRW. This is based on the national average of ~707 million KRW per group, but our group's location in a non-metropolitan region (Jeju) and our field (social sciences/humanities) typically receive lower funding than science/engineering groups. The prior posts from Busan (1.2B), Daejeon (1.3-1.4B), and Hanyang (1.3B) are from engineering or science fields at larger universities, which likely have higher budgets. Our group has 40 graduate scholarship slots: roughly 18 master's (700K/month), 14 doctoral candidates (1M/month), and 8 doctoral students (1.3M/month), totaling about 37 million KRW per month or 444 million KRW annually. This fits within our budget after accounting for 4 postdoc positions at 3M/month (144M/year) and operating costs capped at 10% (110M). The number of slots is limited by our field (political science) and the need to maintain quality; we cannot exceed the support upper limit set by the NRF. I disagree with the higher estimates from other participants because they are in higher-funded STEM fields at larger universities.
evidence: The national average budget per education research group is about 707 million KRW (408,080 million KRW / 577 groups), but social sciences/humanities groups typically receive less than STEM groups.

Our group's location in Jeju, a non-metropolitan region, may qualify for some regional priority, but the field (political science) generally has lower funding ceilings than engineering or natural sciences.

With 40 graduate slots at typical stipend levels (master's 700K, doctoral candidate 1M, doctoral 1.3M), the annual scholarship cost is about 444 million KRW, which is consistent with a 1.1 billion KRW total budget after postdoc salaries and operating costs.

The prior estimates from Busan, Daejeon, and Hanyang are from STEM fields at larger universities, which have higher funding levels; our social science group at a regional university is likely funded lower.
